questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Fool’s Paradise
options: img {max-width:100% !important} Be happy for foolish reasons
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} Fool’s Paradise – Be happy for foolish and unfounded reasons.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The government will ………………… the law from the next month.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} aImplement – put a decision or a plan into effect.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A group of birds travelling together
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} Flock – a group of birds of one kind resting or travelling together.Crowd – a large no of people gathered in a disorganized way.Herd – a large group of hoofed animals.Brood – a group of birds produced from one hatching or birth.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Given below are four sentences which are jumbled. Pick the option that gives their correct order. A. India’s robust street dogs also challenge the supposed superiority of pedigree that dominates dog breeding today B. One of us recently adopted a street dog from Romania C. Bell Kanmani was brought to the United Kingdom by one of the many charities picking up street dogs there and finding them new homes abroad D. While walking her in the UK, Bell Kanmani’s human is regularly confronted with the question ‘What breed is your dog?
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} d

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Plinth
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} c

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Identify the segment in the sentence which contains the grammatical error. Alex picked up/ the boxes /quite easy /even though they were heavy.
options: img {max-width:100% !important} even though they were heavy
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} easily in place of easy as here, we will use adverb to describe the action of picking up. Easy is an adjective which will be wrong to qualify an action

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Luck of the devil
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} c

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} The following sentence has been split into segments. One of them may contain an error. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer. Sunil refused to / attend the meeting / of the association on Saturday.
options: img {max-width:100% !important} of the association on Saturday
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} no error.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Fragile
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} Fragile – easily broken or damaged. Frantic – distraught with fear anxiety, or other emotion Durable – long lasting Sturdy - strongly built

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the highlight segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No substitution required’. As I heard the lecture it reminded me of a book I read two years ago.
options: img {max-width:100% !important} I had read two years ago
img {max-width:100% !important} I was reading two years ago
img {max-width:100% !important} I did read two years ago
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} In the given sentence, there are two events both in the past. The recent one is the hearing of lecture hence it should be in past indefinite, the second one should be in the past perfect as the action relates to older time.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Flexible
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} Flexible – easily bent, pliableBending – easily bentEven – uniformSmooth – even and regular surface.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Flippant
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} Flippant – not showing respect Cheeky - amusingly disrespectful and impoliteSmooth – even and uniform.Polite – humble in speakingTasty - delicious

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. She is holding free English classes for the poor.
options: img {max-width:100% !important} Free English classes have been held for the poor by her.
img {max-width:100% !important} Free English classes are being held by the poor for her.
img {max-width:100% !important} Free English classes are being held for the poor by her.
img {max-width:100% !important} The poor are holding free English classes for her.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} the sentence is in the present continuous tense. To solve such questions easily, just focus on the verb. Here, the sentence is in the present continuous tense, so the verb will change to the form being + V3. Option c shows that.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect speech. She said to him, “May God shower His blessings on you!
options: img {max-width:100% !important} She wished that God might shower His blessings on him.
img {max-width:100% !important} She wished that God may shower His blessings on you.
img {max-width:100% !important} She wished that God might shower His blessings on you.
img {max-width:100% !important} She wished that may God shower your blessings on Him.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} According to the rule:The reporting verb “said to him” – “wished” because it is a prayerModal verb “may” – “might” because the tense is pastYou – “him” as you is the object

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. Ask him to leave the task.
options: img {max-width:100% !important} He should be asked to leave the task.
img {max-width:100% !important} He should be asking to leave the task.
img {max-width:100% !important} He should have been asked to leave the task.
img {max-width:100% !important} He should be ask to leave the task.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} him is the object, its subject will be he, the verb will change from v1 to v3 and ask should be changed to past participle i.e. asked

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. Belief that the most important thing in the world is pleasure
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} Impalpable – incapable of being perceived by touchImponderable – that which cannot be guessed or calculatedImpiety – lack of respect or religious reverence

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} necessary

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} Select the most appropriate option to substitute the highlight segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No substitution required’. This is one of the most remarkable case of all.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer. This hall is / badly in need / to repair.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} This hall is badly in need of repair.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. I decided to stay away from television (1) ______ a whole year. After a week, I started feeling (2) ______. But, my friend Bess cheered me up. She (3) ______ me every day and would take me places. It was (4) ______ how much fun we had without TV. My life had become more (5) ______ without the good old idiot box. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank no. 1.
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} “for a year” will be correct as the particular point of time is not given hence, we cannot use since.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. I decided to stay away from television (1) ______ a whole year. After a week, I started feeling (2) ______. But, my friend Bess cheered me up. She (3) ______ me every day and would take me places. It was (4) ______ how much fun we had without TV. My life had become more (5) ______ without the good old idiot box. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank no. 2
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} Delighted – extremely happyDeceived – trickedDevoted – fully engagedDepressed – extremely sad The most appropriate option is d.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. I decided to stay away from television (1) ______ a whole year. After a week, I started feeling (2) ______. But, my friend Bess cheered me up. She (3) ______ me every day and would take me places. It was (4) ______ how much fun we had without TV. My life had become more (5) ______ without the good old idiot box. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank no. 3
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} the friend is trying to take him out of the sadness and boredom. Hence the most appropriate word is encouraged.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. I decided to stay away from television (1) ______ a whole year. After a week, I started feeling (2) ______. But, my friend Bess cheered me up. She (3) ______ me every day and would take me places. It was (4) ______ how much fun we had without TV. My life had become more (5) ______ without the good old idiot box. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank no. 4
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} Unhealthy – not good for healthUnlimited – without limitsUnlucky – having bad luckUnbelievable – so great and extreme so that is hard to believe; extraordinaryThe author is trying to say that he got enjoyment without tv that was hard to believe, he is very happy spending time without tv. Hence option d is the most appropriate.

questions.question: img {max-width:100% !important} In the following passage some words have been deleted. Fill in the blanks with the help of the alternatives given. Select the most appropriate option for each number. I decided to stay away from television (1) ______ a whole year. After a week, I started feeling (2) ______. But, my friend Bess cheered me up. She (3) ______ me every day and would take me places. It was (4) ______ how much fun we had without TV. My life had become more (5) ______ without the good old idiot box. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank no. 5
questions.explanation: img {max-width:100% !important} certain – definiteExciting – causing happinessBoring – causing boredomPopular – well knownThe most appropriate option is b.
